name: Walnut Pilaf
recipeIngredient: 2 ounces chopped walnuts (reserve 4 halves for garnish)
1 1/2 cups low-sodium beef broth or vegetable broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Spread walnuts evenly on a cookie sheet; bake for 10-12 minutes until fragrant and lightly toasted. Set aside.
2. Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat for 1 minute. Add mushrooms and onion; cook, stirring often for about 10 minutes, until mushroom liquid has evaporated and onion is golden brown.
3. Add rice, broth, vinegar, bitters, rosemary, and pepper to the skillet; bring to a boil.
4. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 40 minutes until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed.
5. Add reserved walnuts; toss to combine. Sprinkle with parsley and garnish with walnut halves before serving.
Each serving (1 cup) provides:
1 fat allowance, 2-1/4 vegetables, 1/2 protein, 1 bread, 10 carbohydrates
Per serving:
231 calories, 6 grams protein, 30 grams carbohydrates
10 grams fat: 6 grams polyunsaturated, 2 grams monounsaturated, 1 gram saturated
10 grams sodium, 0 milligrams cholesterol
